rules.message: HOUSE-CONVENTION rule, `apiRoutes.get(...)`-registered codebases only: GET route with no cache-strategy marker on the same line. A codebase that never calls `apiRoutes.get(...)` never satisfies this rule's `require_file` gate and is silently unaffected by it — this is not a general HTTP rule (see `auth-gates`/`route-exposure` below for this pack's framework-neutral rules). Add `// cache: <strategy>`, `// no-cache: <reason>`, or `// read-model-ok: <reason>` to the route line.

HTTP route registered under a protected path segment (`/admin/`, `/internal/`) with no witnessed auth evidence — no native middleware/decorator/annotation guard was recognized on this route (Express middleware, NestJS `@UseGuards`/`forRoutes`, Spring method-security annotation, ...), and no adapter injected the `auth-guarded` attribute (Mode B envelope injection). An env check (isProduction/NODE_ENV) gates WHERE code runs, not WHO may call it, so it does not clear this — that axis is `route-exposure`'s. If a guard exists but isn't recognized, inject the `auth-guarded` attribute via a Mode B adapter overlay; otherwise add one. Suppress a vetted case with `// auth-gate-ok` (or `# auth-gate-ok` in Python) on the route's registration line.

HTTP route registered under a dev/debug/internal/test/playground path segment with no guard-hint keyword (dev/debug/internal/env/guard/isProduction/isLocal/NODE_ENV) on its registration line — risks leaking dev tooling to production. If no guard exists, gate it behind an env check; if one already exists but isn't grep-visible, rename/restructure so it is. Suppress a vetted case with `// route-exposure-ok` (or `# route-exposure-ok` in Python).
